threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look shocked at the sight of a fox casually trotting past while you're waiting for your bus at the stop. Welcome to the jungle!
Last night on the 333, a guy serenaded the whole bus with 90s R&B. Even the driver couldn’t help but smile. Only in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a guy in a full suit at 7 AM, casually ordering a veg kebab outside the Späti. Only in Neukölln do you witness such effortless contrasts.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find spontaneous dance parties in the U-Bahn stations, especially on weekends. The vibe is always electric and unexpected.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by how effortlessly they dodge the dog on the tracks at Civic Center, while the newbies just stand there confused.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: I watched a guy with a pet iguana ride the Muni down Van Ness. The iguana seemed more chill than half the passengers.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about hip bars and shops, but the real treasure is the quiet plazas where locals gather, especially in the evening.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their bewilderment at the vagueness of train schedules—any delays are met with pure shock, not the resigned sigh of a local.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: When you still say 'the Tube' instead of 'the Overground' for your commute to Hackney. You don't know you've been spoiled yet.
Last night, a chap on the 333 swore he’d invented a new cocktail called 'The Midnight Brixton,' mixing vodka and energy drink. Didn’t catch his name, but that ride sure was lively!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I found a street vendor selling fresh döner at 3 AM, packed with locals chatting, I knew Neukölln was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find a club that doubles as a swimming pool. Kicking back in the water while techno blasts is a vibe unique to our nights out.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they calmly walk past the loud BART fiasco at Civic Center, like it’s just another day in the city.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Last week, a group of tourists cheered as a Muni driver expertly navigated around a sleeping man on the tracks. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think the best paella is by the beach; they miss the hidden gems in Gràcia where locals cook it with love and the right spices.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a recent arrival by their bewilderment in Gràcia's winding streets, staring at maps like they’re solving a puzzle. Classic.
